page: Amazon Web Services m8i.32xlarge is a general-purpose instance powered by the Intel Xeon 6975P-C processor on the x86_64 architecture. It features 128 virtual CPUs mapped to 64 physical cores with hyper-threading enabled, running at a clock speed of 3.9 GHz. The processor is equipped with a 480 MB L3 cache, 2 MB L2 cache, and 48 KB L1d cache. Memory capacity stands at 512.0 GB of DDR5 RAM operating at 7 GHz, providing 4.0 GB of memory per core. This instance does not include local storage or dedicated GPUs, and it operates on the Nitro hypervisor with a baseline network bandwidth of 50 Gbps.

Benchmark results place the m8i.32xlarge in the top tier for multi-core processing, including stress-ng multi-core CPU performance and Geekbench multi-core scores. Its memory subsystem achieves top-tier read and write bandwidth, with cached read speeds reaching 10241.33 GB/sec and uncached large-block reads at 392.26 GB/sec. The inclusion of Advanced Matrix Extensions and Advanced Vector Extensions drives top-tier performance in extended instructions, ray tracing, and LLM inference prompt processing across small, medium, and large models. Single-core performance, however, sits in the average tier, as demonstrated by stress-ng single-core and Geekbench single-core metrics.

From a qualitative cost-efficiency perspective, the m8i.32xlarge offers high resource density for workloads requiring massive parallel processing and high memory bandwidth without the added cost of dedicated GPU hardware. The balanced 1:4 vCPU-to-RAM ratio makes it highly suitable for enterprise-grade applications. Recommended workloads include multi-threaded software compilation, high-throughput Redis database operations, large-scale static web serving, text and image processing, and CPU-based LLM inference tasks.
description: Amazon Web Services m8i.32xlarge is a general-purpose instance featuring 128 vCPUs powered by an Intel Xeon 6975P-C processor running at 3.9 GHz. Built on the x86_64 architecture and the Nitro hypervisor, it includes 512.0 GB of DDR5 memory at 7 GHz, but lacks local storage and GPU accelerators. The instance delivers top-tier multi-core performance, exceptional memory bandwidth, and strong cryptographic capabilities, aided by AVX-512 and AMX instruction sets. While single-core performance remains average, its multi-threaded capabilities, 50 Gbps network bandwidth, and high memory throughput make it highly efficient for demanding workloads. It is optimized for database operations, multi-threaded software compilation, static web serving, and CPU-based LLM inference.
og_description: Amazon Web Services m8i.32xlarge is a general-purpose instance featuring 128 vCPUs, 512 GB DDR5 RAM, and a 3.9 GHz Intel Xeon processor, delivering top-tier multi-core and memory bandwidth performance.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services m8i.32xlarge is a general-purpose instance with 128 vCPUs, 512 GB DDR5 RAM, and a 3.9 GHz Intel Xeon CPU on the Nitro hypervisor.
tagline: A high-density general-purpose instance featuring 128 vCPUs, DDR5 memory, and advanced matrix extensions for parallel computing and database workloads.
bullet_points: 128 vCPUs and 512 GB of high-speed DDR5 memory
Intel Xeon 6975P-C processor with AMX and AVX-512 support
Top-tier multi-core CPU and memory bandwidth performance
50 Gbps baseline network bandwidth on the Nitro hypervisor
Suited for database operations, software compilation, and LLM prompt processing
